messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS\n\nOriginal request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui\nClarifications:\n- framework: Express\n- styling: Tailwind CSS",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "initialize-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository",
      "description": "Create a new project folder, initialize git (optional) and run npm init to produce a package.json file.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Project folder exists with package.json, README placeholder, and (optionally) a git repository initialized."
    },
    {
      "id": "install-dependencies",
      "title": "Install runtime and build dependencies",
      "description": "Install Express and dev/build tools (tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer) and development helpers like nodemon; add npm scripts for start and dev.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Dependencies are listed in package.json and can be installed via npm install; npm scripts start and dev exist and run without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind and CSS build",
      "description": "Initialize Tailwind, create tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js, add input CSS with @tailwind directives, and set up a build script to produce public/styles.css.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind build produces a usable CSS file in the public folder and the HTML can load it to apply Tailwind utilities."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-express-server",
      "title": "Create Express server to serve static site",
      "description": "Create a minimal Express app (server.js or index.js) that serves the compiled Tailwind assets and an index.html from a public directory and listens on a configurable port.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "initialize-project",
        "install-dependencies"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Express server starts and serves index.html and styles.css at http://localhost:<port> returning HTTP 200 for assets."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-ui",
      "title": "Build frontend UI with color controls",
      "description": "Create public/index.html and public/app.js that display the 'hello world' text and provide UI controls (color input(s) and/or dropdowns) to change text and background colors live using Tailwind utility classes or inline styles.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-express-server"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "The page shows \"hello world\" and color controls; interacting with controls immediately updates the displayed text color/background in the browser."
    },
    {
      "id": "integrate-persistence-and-preview",
      "title": "Add persistence and live-preview behavior",
      "description": "Implement client-side persistence (localStorage) so color choices persist across reloads and ensure preview updates are smooth; optionally add a simple Express API endpoint to save preferences server-side if required.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Selected colors persist after page reload; if server persistence implemented, a saved state is retrievable via the API."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test application and write documentation",
      "description": "Manually test flows (changing colors, persistence, server start) and create a README with setup, build, and run instructions plus how to modify the text and colors.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "integrate-persistence-and-preview"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All manual test cases pass and README contains reproducible setup and usage steps so another developer can run the app locally."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down incremental delivery: set up project and dependencies first, configure Tailwind and build pipeline, create the Express server to serve static assets, then implement the frontend UI that manipulates the 'hello world' text colors, add persistence and live-preview, and finish with testing and documentation. Each subtask depends only on prerequisites and is independently verifiable."
}
